diagrams.title: DML Operations Overview
diagrams.description: A comprehensive diagram showing a sample database table and how each of the four DML operations (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) affects the data. The diagram displays a 'Employees' table with columns (ID, Name, Department, Salary) and demonstrates the before/after state for each operation with visual indicators showing data flow and transformation.
elements: Sample Employees table with 5 sample records
SELECT operation block showing retrieval of specific columns with WHERE clause
INSERT operation block showing new row addition highlighted in green
UPDATE operation block showing modification of existing record highlighted in yellow
DELETE operation block showing row removal with strikethrough effect
Result sets displayed for each operation
diagrams.purpose: Provides a holistic view of all four DML commands, helping learners visualize what each operation does to database data and understand the differences between them

diagrams.title: SELECT Query Execution Flow
diagrams.description: A step-by-step flow diagram showing how a SELECT query processes and retrieves data. The diagram illustrates the logical order of query execution: FROM → WHERE → SELECT → ORDER BY, with a sample SQL query flowing through each stage and transforming the result set progressively.
elements: Source table (Employees) as the starting point
FROM clause pointing to the source table
WHERE clause filter box showing condition (Department = 'Sales')
SELECT clause projection box showing selected columns (Name, Salary)
ORDER BY clause sorting box showing ascending order
Final result set display with 2 rows
Arrow flow connecting each processing stage
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand the logical processing sequence of a SELECT query, making it easier to write efficient queries and debug issues

diagrams.title: UPDATE and DELETE with WHERE Clause
diagrams.description: A diagram comparing UPDATE and DELETE operations, emphasizing the critical role of the WHERE clause. It shows a table with rows highlighted to indicate which records will be affected, demonstrating both correct (specific WHERE) and incorrect (missing WHERE) scenarios to prevent accidental data modification.
elements: Employees table with all rows visible
UPDATE statement with WHERE clause (SET Salary = Salary * 1.1)
DELETE statement with WHERE clause (WHERE Department = 'IT')
Highlighted rows showing which records match the WHERE condition (yellow)
Highlighted rows showing which records will be modified/deleted (red outline)
Warning icon showing danger of missing WHERE clause
Before and After comparison panels
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the importance of WHERE clause in UPDATE and DELETE operations, preventing common mistakes that could affect all rows in a table
